headlines.current: I build polished web experiences with a foundation in interactive systems and game development.
headlines.concise: I build polished front-end experiences with an interactive mindset.
headlines.systems: Front-end development shaped by systems thinking, motion, and interaction.
headlines.direct: Polished front-end work with design sensitivity and interactive depth.
headlines.editorial: Front-end experiences shaped by interaction, systems, and visual refinement.

supportCopy.current: I'm a Los Angeles-based developer with roots in game development, years of coding instruction, and a current focus on modern front-end work using React. My work blends design sensitivity, custom logic, and interaction-driven thinking.
supportCopy.trimmed: Los Angeles-based, with roots in games and years of coding instruction, I now focus on React-driven front-end work that blends design sensitivity, custom logic, and interaction-first thinking.
supportCopy.minimal: Los Angeles-based developer focused on modern front-end work, interaction systems, and polished implementation.

about.heading: A developer shaped by games, teaching, and design-driven web work.

full.primary: I started coding through game development when I was young, building projects from scratch and learning how systems, movement, physics, and player experience fit together. Later, I taught coding through youth programs and developed web projects for clients, which pushed me to think more deeply about clarity, usability, and communication. Today, I focus on front-end development and interactive experiences that feel polished, thoughtful, and alive.
full.secondary: My background gives me a mix of strengths that doesn't come from one lane alone: visual problem-solving, custom logic, interface design, and an instinct for how people move through interactive systems.

balanced.primary: I started coding through games, later taught programming through youth programs, and eventually moved into client-facing web work. That combination shaped how I think about systems, usability, communication, and polish.
balanced.secondary: Today I focus on front-end development and interactive experiences that balance clarity, visual judgment, and custom logic.

reduced.primary: Games, teaching, and web development each shaped how I approach front-end work: systems-minded, user-aware, and focused on polish.

focusAreas.label: Application UI
focusAreas.title: Web Applications
focusAreas.body: React-driven interfaces, product-style UI, responsive systems, and polished implementation built for real use rather than one-off visuals.

focusAreas.label: Interaction Systems
focusAreas.title: Game Development
focusAreas.body: Games were the original training ground: systems thinking, interaction design, custom logic, motion, and the instinct to make complex behavior feel natural.

focusAreas.label: Education Practice
focusAreas.title: Teaching / Education
focusAreas.body: Years of coding instruction shaped how I structure information, communicate clearly, and design experiences that are approachable without feeling oversimplified.

direct.heading: Featured Web Projects
direct.intro: Three portfolio-facing web builds that show how I approach front-end implementation, structured content systems, and more product-like application thinking.

delivery.heading: Web Projects Focused On Delivery
delivery.intro: A tighter proof section focused on shipping polished interfaces, turning design direction into dependable front-end systems, and keeping content maintainable after launch.

product.heading: Web Work With Product Thinking
product.intro: This version frames the section less like a gallery and more like a set of interface case studies: CMS builds, education-facing communication, and fuller-stack workflow tools.

selective.heading: A Smaller Set Of Stronger Signals
selective.intro: This version treats the section as a deliberate proof cut: fewer assumptions about breadth, more emphasis on why each project earns its place in the story.

casebook.heading: Three Projects, Three Different Kinds Of Proof
casebook.intro: A casebook-style framing that centers what each piece demonstrates: front-end delivery, communication-heavy design, and more operational application thinking.

featured.title: Artist Foundation Website
featured.meta: Live Site - React - Sanity CMS - Front-End Development
featured.imageAlt: Screenshot of the Artist Foundation Website project
featured.previewContext: Responsive publishing flow with reusable editorial blocks
featured.summary: A live CMS-driven website built with React and Sanity, focused on translating a refined visual direction into a reliable, client-manageable experience.
featured.compactSummary: Live React and Sanity build centered on polished front-end implementation and maintainable editing workflows.
featured.outcome: Shows production delivery: design interpretation, CMS structure, responsive QA, and a finished public-facing launch.
overview: This project focused on turning a visually refined direction into production-ready front-end code without flattening the original design intent.
The build emphasized maintainable CMS modeling, responsive behavior, and editing flexibility so non-technical stakeholders could manage the site after handoff.
contributions: Front-end architecture and component implementation in React
Design collaboration and visual fidelity refinement
Sanity CMS integration for structured client-managed content

featured.title: Code Teaching Program Website
featured.meta: Education Site - Content Strategy - Design + Development
featured.imageAlt: Screenshot of the Code Teaching Program Website project
featured.previewContext: Program clarity, trust-building, and a cleaner enrollment path
featured.summary: A business site for coding instruction work, shaped to communicate programs, teaching experience, and the story behind a youth summer tech offering without losing clarity.
featured.compactSummary: Education-facing site balancing teaching credibility, program explanation, and a simple user flow.
featured.outcome: Shows communication-heavy UI work: shaping information architecture, balancing storytelling with utility, and designing for quick trust.
overview: This site balanced practical program information with storytelling around teaching experience, outcomes, and long-term work with young learners.
Content structure was designed to support trust-building while keeping the page highly scannable for parents, students, and program partners.
contributions: Content architecture and UX flow planning

featured.title: Program Operations Platform
featured.meta: Full-Stack Web App - Admin Workflows - Dashboard UI
featured.imageAlt: Screenshot used for the Program Operations Platform project card
featured.previewContext: Scheduling, admin views, and structured operational data
featured.summary: A product-style application concept for program operations, exploring authenticated dashboards, scheduling workflows, and interface systems that need more than a marketing-site mindset.
featured.compactSummary: Full-stack application direction centered on dashboard structure, workflow clarity, and more complex data states.
featured.outcome: Broadens the story beyond brochure-style websites and signals comfort with application UI, state, and workflow-heavy problem solving.
overview: This concept exists to represent the fuller-stack side of the portfolio: interfaces with denser data, sharper hierarchy needs, and more operational logic.
The emphasis is less on visual flourish and more on making structured information, actions, and progress states easy to understand.
contributions: Dashboard information hierarchy and task flow design
Component system planning for repeated admin patterns

ui.embeddedVideoNote: Embedded walkthrough video with instant stop on close.

roots.heading: Games & Interactive Work
roots.intro: A selection of projects that reflect my roots in game development and my continued interest in systems, motion, and interaction design.
roots.legacyIntro: Before moving into professional web work, I spent years building games from scratch, handling gameplay logic, custom physics, and much of the art and animation myself.

current.heading: Current Interactive Work And Game Roots
current.intro: This section connects current interactive experiments with the game projects that originally shaped how I think about motion, systems, and user response.
current.legacyIntro: The older projects matter because they built the instincts behind my current work: state, feedback, timing, and how interaction should feel moment to moment.

systems.heading: Gameplay Systems, Motion, And Responsive Interaction
systems.intro: Framed this way, the section reads less like a game archive and more like evidence of how I think through systems, feedback loops, and interactive behavior.
systems.legacyIntro: These projects are older, but each one still shows a useful kind of systems thinking: custom movement, enemy behavior, puzzle logic, and player-facing feedback.

play.heading: Interactive Work With A Stronger Prototyping Lens
play.intro: This version treats the section as a set of interaction prototypes, from a current WebVR build to earlier games that focused on moment-to-moment feel.
play.legacyIntro: The archive here is less about nostalgia and more about the habits it trained: building rules, testing feel, and making mechanics readable under pressure.

featured.title: Rhythm Drumming VR Game
featured.meta: In Development - Web VR - Gameplay Systems
featured.imageAlt: Screenshot of the Rhythm Drumming VR Game project
featured.detail: The project explores timing accuracy, player feedback, movement rhythm, and a more physical interaction model than the rest of the portfolio currently shows.

legacy.title: Blood Red
legacy.imageAlt: Screenshot of the Blood Red game project
legacy.summary: A zombie survival shooter built from scratch, where I explored hit reactions, dynamic animation combinations, and instant bullet collision detection along a traced line.
legacy.detail: This project was useful for combat feedback, enemy pressure, and the feel of fast-response weapons.

legacy.title: Toxic Tides
legacy.imageAlt: Screenshot of the Toxic Tides game project
legacy.summary: An underwater multidirectional shooter focused on wave survival, enemy variety, and performance optimization for large numbers of active enemies on screen.
legacy.detail: It pushed on pacing under load, handling multiple threats, and keeping the screen readable during heavier action.

legacy.title: Droid Team
legacy.imageAlt: Screenshot of the Droid Team game project
legacy.summary: A puzzle game built around custom character physics, stacking mechanics, and level design that forced cooperative problem-solving without allowing players to bypass the intended challenge.
legacy.detail: This one shows a different side of the work: puzzle communication, rule clarity, and constraint-driven design.

capabilities.heading: Capabilities
capabilities.intro: Selected projects shown here represent a focused sample of my work. Additional experience details and samples are available upon request.

strengths.heading: How I Tend To Work
strengths.intro: This version treats the section less like a resume list and more like a compact map of the strengths that recur across the rest of the portfolio.

toolkit.heading: A Practical Toolkit Across Front-End, Systems, And Communication
toolkit.intro: A more direct framing that highlights the methods, tools, and working habits behind the project sections above.

proof.heading: A Broader View Of The Work Behind The Projects
proof.intro: Instead of repeating the project sections, this framing shows the supporting range behind them: implementation, design judgment, systems thinking, and communication.

groups.title: Full Stack Web Development
groups.label: Product-minded build work
groups.summary: I build complete web experiences across interface development, application structure, responsive systems, and the integration work needed to ship something stable and usable.

groups.title: Educator / Coding Curriculum
groups.label: Clear technical teaching
groups.summary: My teaching background includes coding instruction, curriculum development, and breaking complex ideas into approachable lessons without flattening the real substance.

groups.title: App And Game Development
groups.label: Systems and interaction
groups.summary: Apps and games have been the strongest training ground for systems thinking, interaction design, mechanics, and the kind of iteration required to make behavior feel right.

direct.heading: Let's build something thoughtful, polished, and interactive.
direct.body: I'm interested in front-end, UI, and interactive development work where design quality, usability, and implementation all matter.
direct.availability: Available for front-end, interactive, and creative development opportunities.
direct.footerLine: Built with React, TypeScript, and custom motion.

conversational.heading: If the work feels aligned, let's talk.
conversational.body: I'm interested in roles and collaborations where front-end craft, interaction quality, and clear implementation all need to work together.
conversational.availability: Open to front-end, interactive, and design-sensitive development work.
conversational.footerLine: Designed and built in React, TypeScript, and custom motion systems.

opportunities.heading: Open to thoughtful front-end and interactive work.
opportunities.body: This framing is more direct about availability, with a stronger emphasis on the kinds of projects and teams I'm interested in joining.
opportunities.availability: Available for front-end, UI, interactive, and creative technology opportunities.
opportunities.footerLine: Portfolio built with React, TypeScript, and custom motion.

editorial.heading: Interested in the intersection of design, systems, and polished front-end execution.
editorial.body: A quieter closing option that reads more like a final note than a sales pitch, while still making contact paths easy to find.
editorial.availability: Available for select front-end and interactive opportunities.
editorial.footerLine: Built in React and TypeScript with custom motion and section-level prototypes.

footerLineVariants.simple: Minimal footer with direct contact links.
footerLineVariants.network: Direct links for email, LinkedIn, and GitHub.
footerLineVariants.build: Built with React, TypeScript, and custom motion.

developerPanel.title: Developer Panel
developerPanel.helperText: Toggle with Shift + D. Variants persist in local storage so prototype choices survive reloads.

controls.label: Hero headline
controls.description: Controls the first-read density and tone of the main hero statement.

options.label: Current long-form
options.description: Keeps the original more descriptive hero statement.

options.label: Concise recommended
options.description: Shorter, cleaner headline for stronger first-glance impact.

options.label: Systems-forward
options.description: Emphasizes interactive and technical foundations more directly.

options.label: Direct design-led
options.description: A cleaner, more immediate line focused on visual polish and depth.

options.label: Editorial hero-first
options.description: A more refined hero statement with slightly more design-forward phrasing.

options.label: Compact
options.description: Very short — just 4 words focusing on core message.

options.label: Minimal with context
options.description: Brief headline mentioning game development roots (8 words).

options.label: Brief descriptive
options.description: Short but descriptive, emphasizing polished and interaction-driven (5 words).

controls.label: Hero supporting copy
controls.description: Controls paragraph density beneath the headline.

options.label: Current paragraph
options.description: Uses the fuller supporting introduction currently on the page.

options.label: Trimmed paragraph
options.description: Tighter support copy for a more editorial opening screen.

options.label: Minimal paragraph
options.description: A very reduced support line for testing a cleaner opening composition.

controls.label: Hero layout
controls.description: Controls how content and animation are arranged in the hero section to give the square animation appropriate space.

options.label: Balanced split
options.description: Current layout with slightly text-favored columns (1.1fr text / 0.9fr animation).

options.label: Balanced overlap
options.description: A close clone of balanced split with a larger animation drifting behind the text column.

options.label: Balanced overlap strong
options.description: Pushes the same overlap idea further with a much larger animation spilling farther behind the text.

options.label: Balanced sticky parallax
options.description: Keeps the split structure but top-aligns the animation, holds it sticky, and adds scroll parallax on larger screens.

options.label: Extreme parallax
options.description: Animation scrolls significantly slower than the page creating a strong depth effect without sticky positioning.

options.label: Extreme parallax fade
options.description: Same slow scroll parallax as extreme version, intended to pair with the About solid-cover tone instead of fading the hero visual itself.

options.label: Extreme parallax fade 2
options.description: A smoother Framer Motion version of the fixed extreme parallax treatment, intended to pair with the About solid-cover tone.

options.label: Animation large
options.description: Shifts balance to give the square animation more presence (0.9fr text / 1.1fr animation).

options.label: Stacked priority
options.description: Vertical stack with animation below text, emphasizing content hierarchy first.

options.label: Animation dominant
options.description: Makes animation significantly larger while keeping text compact and efficient.

options.label: Centered showcase
options.description: Centers both elements with animation positioned prominently for maximum visual impact.

options.label: Header split actions
options.description: Title spans the top, text and animation sit side-by-side below, actions centered at bottom.

options.label: Title priority split
options.description: Large title on top with full attention, balanced content and animation columns, unified actions below.

options.label: Unified top columns
options.description: Compact header at top, subtitle and animation as balanced columns, actions span the bottom.

controls.label: Resume placement
controls.description: Decides whether Resume appears in the hero CTA row or is reserved for later.

options.label: Show in hero
options.description: Keep Resume as a tertiary text link in the hero.

options.label: Hide from hero
options.description: Remove Resume from the hero to reduce density.

controls.label: Navbar style
controls.description: Controls the navigation bar visual treatment and positioning behavior.

options.label: Floating capsule
options.description: Current floating rounded navbar with inset positioning and glass effects.

options.label: Fixed edge
options.description: Traditional full-width navbar fixed to the top edge of the viewport.

options.label: Minimal clean
options.description: Simplified navbar with reduced decoration and cleaner appearance.

options.label: Compact dense
options.description: Smaller, more condensed navbar with tighter spacing throughout.

options.label: Bold accent
options.description: Stronger accent colors and more prominent visual presence.

controls.label: About copy mode
controls.description: Controls how much narrative weight the About section carries.

options.label: Full narrative
options.description: Keeps the current fuller two-paragraph structure.

options.label: Balanced hierarchy
options.description: Reduces reading weight while keeping the section credible and complete.

options.label: Reduced narrative
options.description: Compresses About into a more editorial summary.

controls.label: About layout
controls.description: Changes the overall presentation of the three About focus areas.

options.label: Three-column grid
options.description: Balanced three-up layout for the core pillars.

options.label: Featured lead card
options.description: Makes the first pillar dominant while the other two support it.

options.label: Editorial list
options.description: Reduces card heaviness and moves toward a lighter editorial feel.

options.label: Split feature rows
options.description: Reshapes the area into wide horizontal feature rows with stronger reading rhythm.

options.label: Accent rail stack
options.description: Keeps the vertical stacked rhythm but treats each pillar as a separate lane with accent rails instead of steps.

options.label: Split panel stack
options.description: Uses wide split cards with a stronger left label column and a cool accent seam.

options.label: Beacon cards
options.description: Stacks independent cards with compact accent markers and more glow-driven emphasis.

controls.label: Section tone
controls.description: Controls how strongly the About section separates from surrounding sections.

options.label: Neutral surface
options.description: Minimal tonal distinction from the surrounding page.

options.label: Elevated surface
options.description: Adds a subtler raised-surface feel behind the section.

options.label: Chapter separation
options.description: Creates a stronger chapter break before proof-of-work sections.

options.label: Solid cover
options.description: Uses an opaque About surface with a top cover edge so the section cleanly hides the hero visual as it scrolls in.

controls.label: Background style
controls.description: Changes the overall page background composition.

options.label: Minimal field
options.description: Very restrained background with only a faint ambient glow.

options.label: Top halo
options.description: A centered halo glow similar to the current direction.

options.label: Ambient mesh
options.description: Multiple soft glows distributed across the page.

options.label: Aurora wash
options.description: Diagonal luminous washes with a more atmospheric feel.

options.label: High contrast stage
options.description: Darker base with stronger spotlight separation.

options.label: Ink field
options.description: Moodier background with off-center illuminated pockets.

controls.label: Grid overlay
controls.description: Controls whether the page background includes a grid and what kind.

options.label: No grid
options.description: Disables the page grid entirely.

options.label: Fine grid
options.description: Subtle medium-density grid.

options.label: Wide grid
options.description: Larger grid cells with a calmer technical feel.

options.label: Blueprint grid
options.description: More visible technical grid with fine subdivisions.

controls.label: Contrast level
controls.description: Controls how strong the background glow and grid read against the page.

options.label: Soft
options.description: More restrained and low-contrast.

options.label: Balanced
options.description: Moderate contrast for general use.

options.label: High
options.description: Stronger contrast and more visible background structure.

controls.label: Background parallax
controls.description: Restores lightweight scroll-reactive movement for the root CSS background only.

options.label: None
options.description: No background parallax movement.

options.label: Soft parallax
options.description: Very light scroll-linked background drift designed to stay inexpensive.

options.label: Depth parallax
options.description: A slightly stronger offset for more visible page-depth motion.

controls.label: Section surfaces
controls.description: Controls how transparent or solid section and card surfaces feel across the page.

options.label: Airy transparency
options.description: Brings back a lighter translucent surface treatment.

options.label: Balanced
options.description: Keeps a moderate amount of transparency without going fully solid.

options.label: Solid surfaces
options.description: Removes most transparency from section and card surfaces for maximum stability.

controls.label: Section atmosphere
controls.description: Controls how much section-level glow and tonal atmosphere is allowed.

options.label: Full atmosphere
options.description: Keeps the richer section glows and tonal accents.

options.label: Reduced atmosphere
options.description: Softens section glow and decorative tonal shifts.

options.label: Atmosphere off
options.description: Removes most section-level atmospheric accent layers.

controls.label: Glass intensity
controls.description: Reintroduces backdrop-filter at controlled levels so you can test glass without forcing the heaviest version everywhere.

options.label: Off
options.description: Keeps the current opaque performance-safe surfaces with no live glass blur.

options.label: Subtle glass
options.description: A light blur and tint reduction intended to stay the safest visually and technically.

options.label: Medium glass
options.description: A more noticeable frosted treatment with moderate blur and stronger translucency.

options.label: Strong glass
options.description: The richest glass treatment available in this setup and the most likely to cost performance.

controls.label: Glass behavior
controls.description: Controls whether the UI stays on a high-quality fake glass treatment or restores real backdrop blur after scrolling settles.

options.label: Fake glass only
options.description: Uses the glass look without live backdrop blur, which is the safest performance choice.

options.label: Idle real, cached snapshot
options.description: Captures the navbar background while idle, then uses that cached image during scroll before restoring real blur once idle again.

options.label: Idle real, smooth fade
options.description: Transitions the navbar between fake glass during motion and real glass when idle with a softer visual handoff.

options.label: Always real
options.description: Keeps live backdrop blur active on every eligible surface and is the most expensive option.

controls.label: Glass transition length
controls.description: Controls how long the navbar takes to ease back into real glass once scrolling has fully settled.

options.label: Quick
options.description: A shorter return transition with less lingering motion.

options.label: Balanced
options.description: A moderate return transition suitable for most combinations.

options.label: Slow
options.description: A longer return transition with a more noticeable handoff.

options.label: Cinematic
options.description: The longest return transition for a very soft glass re-entry.

controls.label: Idle pause before glass
controls.description: Controls how long scrolling must stay settled before the navbar returns to real glass, which also debounces small scroll interruptions.

options.label: Short pause
options.description: Returns to real glass sooner after motion stops.

options.label: Balanced pause
options.description: A moderate pause that avoids most rapid toggling.

options.label: Long pause
options.description: Waits longer before restoring real glass so tiny scrolls do not retrigger it constantly.

options.label: Linger
options.description: The most debounced option, keeping fake glass active until scrolling has been clearly idle for a while.

controls.label: Glass scope
controls.description: Controls whether glass is limited to persistent chrome or allowed onto section surfaces too.

options.label: Chrome only
options.description: Applies glass only to the navbar, mobile menu, and developer panel.

options.label: Visible sections
options.description: Applies section glass only while a section is in view, while chrome stays glassy.

options.label: All eligible surfaces
options.description: Applies glass to both chrome and the main eligible content surfaces across the page.

controls.label: Contact framing
controls.description: Changes the tone of the closing call-to-action section.

options.label: Direct contact
options.description: Keeps the straightforward current call-to-action tone.

options.label: Conversational
options.description: Feels more like an invitation to talk than a formal CTA.

options.label: Opportunities
options.description: Makes availability and role fit more explicit.

options.label: Editorial closing note
options.description: A quieter and more restrained final section tone.

controls.label: Contact layout
controls.description: Changes the structure of the closing call-to-action block.

options.label: Centered CTA
options.description: Keeps the current centered closing composition.

options.label: Split closing
options.description: Uses a left-right split between message and actions.

options.label: Stacked note
options.description: A more linear, editorial closing stack with quieter pacing.

controls.label: Contact surface
controls.description: Changes the visual atmosphere of the contact section.

options.label: Ambient glow
options.description: Keeps the current soft atmospheric background.

options.label: Accent glow
options.description: Increases the cyan focus and closing-stage energy.

options.label: Quiet surface
options.description: Pulls the atmosphere back for a calmer final section.

controls.label: Footer tone
controls.description: Adjusts the footer line emphasis beneath the contact section.

options.label: Simple footer
options.description: Minimal footer emphasis.

options.label: Network footer
options.description: Leans on the outbound link set more clearly.

options.label: Build note footer
options.description: Keeps the footer as a concise build note.

controls.label: Section framing
controls.description: Changes how the Capabilities section is positioned in relation to the rest of the page.

options.label: Capabilities
options.description: Keeps the current straightforward capabilities framing.

options.label: Working strengths
options.description: Makes the section feel more like a concise map of recurring strengths.

options.label: Toolkit
options.description: Frames the section as a more practical tool-and-method overview.

options.label: Supporting proof
options.description: Positions the section as broader context behind the project work.

controls.label: Layout mode
controls.description: Changes the structure of the capabilities groups.

options.label: Balanced grid
options.description: A clear four-card grid with even weight across groups.

options.label: Featured lead
options.description: Gives the first group stronger hierarchy and lets the others support it.

options.label: Horizontal bands
options.description: Turns the section into stacked bands with stronger label-to-content separation.

options.label: Editorial list
options.description: A calmer list-like layout with less card-block repetition.

controls.label: Skill item treatment
controls.description: Changes how the individual capability items are displayed inside each group.

options.label: Chips
options.description: Keeps the current chip-based readout.

options.label: Minimal list
options.description: Removes most chip styling for a cleaner, lower-noise read.

options.label: Grouped stack
options.description: Uses denser grouped tags for a more compact technical feel.

controls.label: Section framing
controls.description: Changes how Games & Interactive Work is positioned within the portfolio story.

options.label: Game roots
options.description: Frames the section as the origin of your interactive thinking.

options.label: Current + roots

options.label: Systems-forward

options.label: Play + prototyping

controls.label: Featured project treatment
controls.description: Changes how the current Rhythm Drumming VR project is presented.

options.label: Spotlight card

options.label: Split prototype panel
options.description: Separates meta and summary into a stronger left-right layout.

options.label: Image left 1

controls.label: Legacy archive layout
controls.description: Changes how the older game projects are presented below the featured project.

options.label: Archive grid
options.description: Three-up legacy project cards with equal weight.

options.label: Archive rail
options.description: Wide horizontal project rows for a calmer reading rhythm.

options.label: Signal cards
options.description: Smaller cards with concise project signals and less summary weight.

controls.label: Surface treatment
controls.description: Changes the visual tone of the featured project and archive surfaces.

options.label: Ambient gradients
options.description: Keeps the current atmospheric presentation.

options.label: Framed UI chrome
options.description: Makes the media areas feel more like interface windows or browser frames.

options.label: Blueprint lines
options.description: Leans into grid lines and diagram-like overlays for a more systems-forward tone.

controls.label: Section framing
controls.description: Changes how the Featured Web Projects intro positions the proof section.

options.label: Direct portfolio proof
options.description: Straightforward framing focused on featured work and breadth.

options.label: Delivery-focused
options.description: Emphasizes shipped work, implementation quality, and maintainability.

options.label: Product-thinking
options.description: Frames the section more like application case studies than a gallery.

options.label: Selective proof
options.description: Makes the section feel more curated and deliberate, with less gallery energy.

options.label: Casebook tone
options.description: Leans harder into project reasoning and what each piece demonstrates.

controls.label: Card layout
controls.description: Controls the section rhythm between one dominant feature, a balanced grid, and a more editorial rail.

options.label: Featured-first
options.description: One dominant leading project followed by supporting cards.

options.label: Image left 1
options.description: Keeps the lead project dominant while giving the image a more balanced medium height.

options.label: Balanced grid
options.description: Three cards presented with more equal visual weight.

options.label: Editorial rail
options.description: A calmer stacked layout with more horizontal reading rhythm.

options.label: Staggered mosaic
options.description: Breaks the section into uneven card heights to reduce template-like repetition.

options.label: Showcase split
options.description: Pairs a dominant lead card with a tighter vertical support column.

controls.label: Card detail mode
controls.description: Adjusts how much information is visible before the modal opens.

options.label: Detailed cards
options.description: Shows summary text, tags, and the fuller current card density.

options.label: Compact cards
options.description: Pulls back visible detail so hierarchy comes more from structure.

options.label: Outcome-led cards
options.description: Highlights what each project proves before the modal carries the rest.

options.label: Minimal cards
options.description: Removes most collapsed-card detail so the section breathes more.

options.label: Signal cards
options.description: Uses small proof signals and context chips instead of fuller summaries.

controls.label: Surface treatment
controls.description: Changes the visual character of the project media panels and modal preview surface.

options.label: Ambient gradients
options.description: Keeps the current atmospheric gradient surfaces.

options.label: Framed UI chrome
options.description: Makes the media areas feel more like interface windows or browser frames.

options.label: Blueprint lines
options.description: Leans into grid lines and diagram-like overlays for a more systems-forward tone.

controls.label: Dialog body layout
controls.description: Keeps media full bleed while changing how the text and supporting details are arranged underneath it.

options.label: Stacked narrative
options.description: Single-column body layout with the supporting blocks flowing underneath the main copy.

options.label: Split content
options.description: Main copy on the left with secondary details in a supporting right column.

options.label: Sticky sidebar
options.description: A more editorial layout with the supporting block held in a narrower sticky side rail on larger screens.

controls.label: Dialog size
controls.description: Changes the overall width and visual scale of the shared project popup.

options.label: Compact
options.description: A narrower dialog for tighter reading and less screen takeover.

options.label: Standard
options.description: Balanced default size for most project detail states.

options.label: Expanded
options.description: A wider case-study presentation with more breathing room for content.

selectedWork.heading: Selected Client & Concept Work
selectedWork.intro: Additional projects exploring branding, layout systems, and functional front-end concepts across different audiences and styles.
selectedWork.note: These projects represent selected concept and client-facing work. Additional samples are available upon request.

items.title: Buffalo Soldiers
items.summary: A web concept designed as a centralized hub structure for chapters, balancing organizational clarity with a stronger sense of identity and navigation.

items.title: Inland Empire Institute
items.summary: A site concept for technology certification programs, focused on communicating pathways, legitimacy, and program value in a clear and structured way.

items.title: 8gb
items.summary: A retro-styled e-commerce concept with a stronger visual personality, designed to merge playful aesthetic choices with a usable storefront structure.
